channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for July 4, 2026: Nothing but Fireworks edition
item.description: This week, we celebrate Independence Day with a collection of absolute bangers, including interviews with John Cusack, Kali Reis, Arden Cho, and Aasif Mandvi
item.content:encoded:
This week, we celebrate Independence Day with a collection of absolute bangers, including interviews with John Cusack, Kali Reis, Arden Cho, and Aasif Mandvi

(Image credit: Frazer Harrison)

item.title: Celebrating this July 4 by exploring films and TV shows that portray the American dream
item.description: The U.S. is turning 250 this weekend. To mark the anniversary, we discuss the films and TV series that best depict the American dream.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. is turning 250 this weekend. To mark the anniversary, we discuss the films and TV series that best depict the American dream.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: The Parade of Presidents has been going since 1976. Meet the woman who keeps it going
item.description: On the main street of Cuba City, Wisc., there's a Parade of Presidents, with decorative shields for each President, begun as a bicentennial project in 1976. Donna Rogers keeps it all going.
item.content:encoded: On the main street of Cuba City, Wisc., there's a Parade of Presidents, with decorative shields for each President, begun as a bicentennial project in 1976. Donna Rogers keeps it all going.
item.dc:creator: Susan Bence

item.title: After weeks of speculation, Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wed in New York
item.description: Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.
item.content:encoded:
Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Nearly half of Americans surveyed don't know what America 250 commemorates
item.description: A new national poll reveals a striking paradox in public sentiment ahead of America's 250th anniversary: a disconnect between Americans' strong patriotic pride and their lack of civic knowledge.
item.content:encoded:
A new national poll reveals a striking paradox in public sentiment ahead of America's 250th anniversary: a disconnect between Americans' strong patriotic pride and their lack of civic knowledge.

(Image credit: Juan Mabromata)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
item.description: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
item.content:encoded:
Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.

(Image credit: Hulton Archive)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: How young people feel about American identity, on the nation's 250th birthday
item.description: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.content:encoded: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.dc:creator: Janet W. Lee

item.title: Steven Spielberg on falling in love with movies and becoming a filmmaker
item.description: After making E.T. and Close Encounters of the Third Kind years ago, Spielberg returns to the theme of extraterrestrials in his new film, Disclosure Day. Originally broadcast Nov. 9, 2022.
item.content:encoded: After making E.T. and Close Encounters of the Third Kind years ago, Spielberg returns to the theme of extraterrestrials in his new film, Disclosure Day. Originally broadcast Nov. 9, 2022.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: You know the Mayflower. What about the White Lion? Here's the story of 'Two Ships'
item.description: David S. Reynolds' book examines the twin legacies of the Mayflower, which carried the Pilgrims to Plymouth in 1620, and the White Lion, which brought the first enslaved Africans to Virginia in 1619.
item.content:encoded: David S. Reynolds' book examines the twin legacies of the Mayflower, which carried the Pilgrims to Plymouth in 1620, and the White Lion, which brought the first enslaved Africans to Virginia in 1619.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan

item.title: What Swifties need to know about the wedding of the year
item.description: NPR's A Martinez talks with Bryan West, a reporter who covers Taylor Swift for USA Today and The Tennessean, about what's could be the most anticipated wedding of the summer.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez talks with Bryan West, a reporter who covers Taylor Swift for USA Today and The Tennessean, about what's could be the most anticipated wedding of the summer.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
